summary: Iteration 12 — validated 3 new improvements (Brand Kit 'Inherit from site' font option + alphabetical fonts + deferred creation) and performance (useDeferredValue). 8 of 9 feature areas PASS. 1 CRITICAL BUG FOUND: the 'New page' flow from Dashboard (and PageRail) is completely broken — clicking a template in PageTemplatePicker throws a DataCloneError ('Failed to execute pushState on History: Symbol(react.forward_ref) could not be cloned') and silently fails navigation, leaving the user on the dashboard. Root cause: Dashboard.createPage and PageRail.createFromTemplate pass the full PAGE_TEMPLATES entry (which includes a Lucide-React icon component — a forwardRef symbol) through React Router's navigate(path,{state:{template}}) — browser history state requires structured-cloneable data, and React components are not. As a side-effect, the 'pristine page, no edit → no DB record' test LOOKS like it passes only because navigation never occurs at all; in reality the editor screen is unreachable via the New-page button. Sections flow works perfectly. All other features confirmed green.

ui_bugs.issue: navigate('/edit/page/new', { state: { template } }) throws DataCloneError because the template object contains `icon: <LucideReactComponent>` which is a forwardRef symbol and not structured-cloneable. The navigation silently fails, leaving the user on the dashboard. The 'New page' button is effectively broken for all 7 built-in templates.

action_items: CRITICAL: Fix New-page flow. In Dashboard.createPage and PageRail.createFromTemplate, strip the non-serializable icon field before passing template through router state. Minimal fix: const { icon, ...serializable } = template; navigate('/edit/page/new', { state: { template: serializable } }); — alternatively pass only template.id and have PageEditor re-hydrate via PAGE_TEMPLATES_BY_ID[id]. The latter is cleaner.
Also audit SectionPicker path: createSection passes typeId (string) via the URL ?type=hero, which is already serializable — no fix needed there.
(nit) FontField's data-testid for inherit option uses the string literal 'inherit' as a suffix; if a Google Font were ever named 'Inherit' the testid would collide. Unlikely but worth a comment.

(nit) applyFontToSnippet's inherit branch uses /font-family:\s*"Poppins"[^;}]*/g — this will NOT match font-family stacks that reference Poppins without quotes (e.g. `font-family:Poppins,sans-serif`). Today all snippets generate the quoted form so this is OK, but tighten the regex or add a comment about the quoted-Poppins invariant.
critical_code_review_comments: Router state serialization: passing the entire template object (with React component icon) into React Router v6 state is a latent bug that only surfaced because of Chrome's strict structured-clone enforcement. Rule of thumb: router state must be JSON-serializable. Either pass template.id (preferred — single source of truth) or explicitly pick serializable fields.

Dashboard.jsx.createPage has no try/catch and no user feedback when navigate throws — the DataCloneError goes to the console and the user sees only that the picker closed. Add an error boundary + toast for router-state errors so this class of bug doesn't ship silently again.

Editor.jsx + PageEditor.jsx both use skipNextLoadRef to suppress the load effect after navigate-replace. This ref gates on a boolean, but if the user opens /edit/section/new, types, gets navigated to /edit/section/sec_X, then navigates away and back via browser history, the effect re-runs and is correct — but the ref flip is a stateful side-effect of render. Consider a state machine (pristine|saving|live) for clarity.

applyFontToSnippet handles the inherit sentinel as a string === 'inherit'. Would be safer to compare against the INHERIT_FONT constant (already imported elsewhere as a sentinel) so a future rename ripples cleanly.

useDeferredValue(snippet) is added around the final rendered snippet string, which correctly de-prioritizes iframe re-renders while keeping form inputs snappy. Verified anecdotally by dragging the Logo Strip speed slider — input updates immediately, iframe lags by ~300-500ms. Good tradeoff.

success_rate.backend: 100% (brand-kit round-trip via curl, sections count stable)
success_rate.frontend: ~89% (8/9 feature areas pass; New-page flow broken)

test_credentials: Session token BhDNRIZaNl8EgFgndl8MywKImOdn8PUpyj0deIry1ZYl9fxJ1L0SKFMKYdS1BN_7 valid at time of testing. Brand kit was mutated to heading_font=inherit during testing and RESTORED to factory defaults (Poppins) via PUT /api/brand-kit at end of run.
seed_data_creation: Created and cleaned up 1 test section 'TEST_Deferred_v2' (sec_a7587d66c465) — DELETE returned 200, section count verified stable at 6 before and after. No page seed was possible because the New-page flow is broken.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Validated features (all PASS): (1) Brand Kit dropdown — 'Inherit from site' (italic, testid brand-{heading,body}-font-option-inherit) is first, followed by 12 fonts in strict alphabetical order Inter→Work Sans. Both heading and body dropdowns verified. (2) Inherit applies to snippet — set brand kit heading_font=inherit, opened section sec_bbcda668ac3f editor; rendered snippet (4443 chars) contains 'font-family:inherit' and has NO 'family=Poppins' @import and NO 'font-family:"Poppins"' declaration; iframe srcdoc matches. (3) Deferred section creation — URL is /edit/section/new?type=hero, sections count stable at 6 across pristine landing + back button. No POST /api/sections fires during pristine flow. (4) Deferred section WITH edit — typing 'TEST_Deferred' into section-name-input triggered exactly 1 POST /api/sections, URL replaced to /edit/section/sec_a7587d66c465, subsequent edit '_v2' fired 0 POSTs (silent PATCH via the batch queue — no visible PATCH in network because the edit was batched inside the same save after the POST settled; section content confirmed persisted via GET). (5) Race resilience — rapid typing produced exactly 1 POST (verified via network listener). (6) useDeferredValue works visually on Logo Strip speed slider. (7) Inherit-removal regression — switching back to Inter restores @import url(...family=Inter) and font-family:"Inter" (tested indirectly via brandKit.js source review). (8) Regression — existing iter9/10/11 features unchanged. FAILING feature: New-page flow from Dashboard (and from PageRail inside the page editor) is broken — DataCloneError thrown on navigate with template state. Pristine-page test 'passed' by accident because navigation never happens. Verified against iteration_11.json's test_credentials recipe. Restored brand kit to Poppins.
rca of the issue: React Router v6's navigate(path, { state }) uses the browser History API's pushState/replaceState, which requires state to be structured-cloneable per HTML spec. The PAGE_TEMPLATES entries in /app/frontend/src/sections/pageTemplates.js contain `icon: FileText` (a Lucide-React forwardRef component). Structured-clone chokes on the forwardRef Symbol and throws DataCloneError. Repro: open / → click data-testid='new-page-button' → click any data-testid='template-*' → DevTools console shows 'Failed to execute pushState on History: Symbol(react.forward_ref) could not be cloned'; page stays on Dashboard. Fix path: in Dashboard.jsx line 49-52 (createPage) and PageRail.jsx line 82-88 (createFromTemplate), replace `{ state: { template } }` with `{ state: { template: { id: template.id, name: template.name, blocks: template.blocks } } }` (or pass only `template.id` and re-lookup via PAGE_TEMPLATES_BY_ID in PageEditor). Mitigation: 2-line code change; no schema or API changes needed.
